economic_notes: Old Massett Economic Development Corporation, Old Massett Economic Development Limited Partnership, and 1199196 B.C. Ltd. consolidated. Six GBEs accounted for by modified equity method including Haida Gwaii Forest Products Joint Venture (50% owned), TLL Yahda Energy Ltd., TLL Yahda Energy Limited Partnership, 1319535 B.C. Ltd., 1319535 Limited Partnership, and 1203559 B.C. Ltd. CMHC funding increased significantly from $363,531 to $6,769,409. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing increased from $741,121 to $1,739,278.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2023 (not 2024). CMHC funding increased dramatically year-over-year from $363,531 to $6,769,409, likely reflecting major capital housing projects (assets under construction were $9,068,968 at year-end vs $717,648 prior year). Three entities fully consolidated and six GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. New accounting standards adopted prospectively April 1, 2022 (PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations and PS 3450 Financial Instruments). Deferred revenue increased significantly to $16,343,410. Net financial assets per statement of changes is $6,875,953 (actual) vs budget of $2,047,043. Tangible capital assets net book value derived from accumulated surplus breakdown showing investment in tangible capital assets of $26,283,742.
